criteriaResults.description: All three .ps1 files exist beside their .sh counterparts
criteriaResults.evidence: tests/run-all.ps1, tests/layer2/smoke-test.ps1, tests/layer3/meta-test.ps1 all present in expected locations next to corresponding .sh files.

Ran [System.Management.Automation.Language.Parser]::ParseFile on all three; zero parse errors. Output: 'OK   tests/run-all.ps1' / 'OK   tests/layer2/smoke-test.ps1' / 'OK   tests/layer3/meta-test.ps1'.
head -3 of each file shows the exact three required header lines in order.

criteriaResults.description: AST scan finds no bare invocations of forbidden POSIX tools (bash|sh|wsl|sed|awk|grep|find|realpath|mktemp|dirname|basename|tr|cut|xargs)
criteriaResults.evidence: AST CommandAst scan over all three files returned 'AST scan clean'. Allowed externals (jq, git, bats, powershell) are used; no forbidden tokens appear as bare commands.

criteriaResults.description: CLI parity: each .ps1 accepts the same flags/positionals as its .sh, plus optional --help
criteriaResults.evidence: run-all.sh accepts positional layer (layer1|layer2|layer3|all|--layer1-only); run-all.ps1 handles all five plus --help/-h/-?. smoke-test.sh and meta-test.sh accept no args; .ps1 versions accept only the new --help flag. Unknown layer to run-all.ps1 exits 1 (verified).

criteriaResults.description: --help on each .ps1 exits 0 and prints usage
criteriaResults.evidence: Invoked each with --help. All three printed a usage block and reported EXIT=0.

criteriaResults.description: run-all.ps1 default is layer1; bats invoked on tests/layer1/*.bats; layer2/3 dispatch to .ps1 (not .sh); aggregated exit codes propagate
criteriaResults.evidence: $Layer = 'layer1' default at line 18. Layer 1 builds $batsFiles from Get-ChildItem -Filter '*.bats' and calls 'bats @batsFiles' (line 80). Layer 2 dispatches to tests/layer2/smoke-test.ps1 (line 91-92). Layer 3 dispatches to tests/layer3/meta-test.ps1 (line 103-104). 'all' short-circuits on first non-zero rc. Verified runtime: 'unknown-layer' -> EXIT=1; 'layer2' without guard -> skip + EXIT=0; 'layer3' without guard -> skip + EXIT=0.

criteriaResults.description: smoke-test.ps1 honors HARNESS_SMOKE_TEST=1 guard, mirrors prompt, has equivalent assertion list, cleans up on exit
criteriaResults.evidence: Guard at line 36-39: prints message and exits 0 when env var unset (verified runtime). Prompt mirrors .sh (PowerShell variant of the hello-world CLI). All 9 .sh assertions are reproduced 1:1: product-spec >100 bytes, sprint-plan.json valid, eval-report.json exists, PASS in eval, harness git tag, harness branch, completedSprints, cost-log.json, progress.md contains PASS. Cleanup via try/catch + trap that prints 'Smoke test dir: ... (not cleaned for inspection)' on success and failure.

criteriaResults.description: meta-test.ps1 honors HARNESS_META_TEST=1 guard; assertions mirror .sh; prompt updated to PowerShell artifacts and consistent with harness
criteriaResults.evidence: Guard at line 41-45: prints message and exits 0 when env var unset (verified runtime). Assertions mirror .sh (sprint cycle, sprint passed, test files created, info-only count, generated tests run/exist). Prompt references harness/lib/utils.ps1 with the actual function names (ConvertTo-Slug, Format-SprintNumber, Get-SprintDirectory, Read-JsonField, Test-FileNonEmpty, Initialize-HarnessState, Update-Handoff, Update-RegressionRegistry — all confirmed defined in utils.ps1) and meta-tests/run.ps1 entry point. Hook references remain .sh (hooks are out-of-scope per spec). Generator log section 'Behavioural nuances handled -> Meta test prompt' documents the change. .ps1 also gracefully handles meta-tests/run.sh fallback without invoking bash.

criteriaResults.description: Sprint 01-03 deliverables (harness/lib/*.ps1, harness/orchestrate.ps1) untouched by sprint-04
criteriaResults.evidence: git diff 8b46959..HEAD (sprint-03 merge..tip) -- harness/lib/ harness/orchestrate.ps1 returns empty. git status shows only the three new untracked .ps1 files plus harness-state.
git diff 8b46959..HEAD -- 'tests/*.sh' 'tests/**/*.sh' returns empty. No tracked .sh file was modified.
ls harness/hooks/*.ps1 returns 'No such file or directory'. Only the four original .sh hooks remain in harness/hooks/.

criteriaResults.description: Whole-port coverage: 11 .ps1 at in-scope locations matches 11 .sh
criteriaResults.evidence: Counted: harness/orchestrate.ps1 + 7 harness/lib/*.ps1 + tests/run-all.ps1 + tests/layer2/smoke-test.ps1 + tests/layer3/meta-test.ps1 = 11. Equivalent .sh enumeration also = 11.

regressionResults.evidence: git diff 8b46959..HEAD -- harness/lib/utils.ps1 harness/lib/git.ps1 harness/lib/invoke.ps1 returns empty; sprint-01 deliverables unmodified.
git diff 8b46959..HEAD -- harness/lib/contract.ps1 harness/lib/planner.ps1 harness/lib/generator.ps1 harness/lib/evaluator.ps1 returns empty; sprint-02 deliverables unmodified.
git diff 8b46959..HEAD -- harness/orchestrate.ps1 returns empty; sprint-03 deliverable unmodified.

notes: Behavioral parity is high. The three traps called out in instructions (run-all secretly calling .sh, guard inversion, shorter assertion list, prompt asking for .sh artifacts the harness can't produce) are all avoided.
smoke-test.ps1 prompt was localized to a PowerShell hello-world (.sh asks for bash). This is appropriate for the Windows port and remains a trivial scope project; not a parity issue.

smoke-test.ps1 omits the gtimeout/timeout 1800 wrapper (the .sh used it conditionally on macOS/Linux). Reasonable since native Windows lacks an equivalent CLI; an enhancement could add Start-Process -Wait with a job timeout but is not required by the contract.
meta-test.ps1 prompt restricts file-count search to meta-tests/ (the .sh's -o operator unintentionally globalized one branch); .ps1 is arguably more correct and not a regression.
meta-test.ps1 also adds a 'test-*.ps1' filename pattern alongside the .sh's '*.bats'/'test-*.sh' patterns -- accommodates ported suites without weakening assertions.
